bio: An independent compliance consultant who moves easily between donor boards, galleries, and transport ledgers. Behind the polished surface, she brokers cleaned-up paper trails and quietly trades access to sensitive records.
notes: Recurring suspect and witness across both arcs. C020 reveals her true role as a fixer. C030 confirms she relied on an internal records leak rather than direct control of the disappearances.

bio: Director of the Larkspur Civic Hall donor program. Skilled at public-facing charm, less skilled at keeping duplicate financial trails aligned.
notes: Initial fraud suspect whose sloppy second print run helps open the Glass Ledger arc.

bio: A print broker and document finisher who handles boutique jobs for galleries, private estates, and anyone willing to pay for a convincing duplicate.
notes: Useful connector between forged provenance work and Mira Voss's ledger-cleaning network.

bio: A harbor-market logistics clerk who always has an explanation for missing stock, open lockers, and nighttime access pings.
notes: Reappears deeper in the fraud arc, showing the same alibi pattern in two separate schemes.

bio: Operations lead at a boutique payment startup. Expert enough to hide manual action behind automated language, until the device trail is examined closely.
notes: Good example of the fraud department's digital-trace template.

bio: Payroll auditor for a public benefits annex. Presents as methodical and above suspicion while using routine office movement to hide after-hours edits.
notes: Introduces a bureaucratic version of fraud before the leak thread becomes explicit.

bio: Insurance adjuster known for dramatic field photos and selective timelines. Treats visual evidence as something to compose, not preserve.
notes: Her case leans on physical evidence rather than spreadsheets, broadening the fraud department's evidence mix.

bio: A respected appraiser whose seal and opinion can make or break private sales. She has learned how to weaponize that reputation.
notes: Positions forged art paperwork as part of the wider ledger-cleaning economy.

bio: Treasury analyst for a transit rebate office. Knows the difference between a missing serial gap and one that can be explained away to a rushed supervisor.
notes: Useful midpoint suspect for teaching voucher, batch, and parcel-weight contradictions.

bio: A junior lab assistant who disappears after leaving work with a transit screenshot on her phone and a habit of documenting everything.
notes: Missing person profile rather than culprit. Good early dossier entry for the Missing Persons department.

bio: Manager of the Hearthlight Hostel. Friendly in public, inflexible when asked about room access and overnight movement.
notes: Built to support keycard and hallway-timeline contradictions.

bio: A club singer with a growing local following and a habit of slipping away from handlers the moment a room gets too loud.
notes: Missing person whose backstage movement pulls the recurring suspect into the second arc.

bio: Volunteer search coordinator who likes to be first on scene and first on camera. Comfortable turning search optics into cover.
notes: Good template suspect for route, light, and vehicle-track contradictions.

bio: A building access technician who understands blind spots, maintenance excuses, and exactly how much most people trust a dead camera.
notes: Her case showcases the Missing Persons department's enhance-forward surveillance play.

bio: Dock foreman on the North Quay cold berths. Acts as if every movement on the pier is routine until someone asks for a sequence of times.
notes: Extends the arc toward the waterfront spaces later reused in C028-C030.

bio: Counsellor at the Lantern Youth Shelter. Protective, overextended, and increasingly willing to control the story if she thinks the system will fail a witness.
notes: Her case contains the Missing Persons arc's first overt internal-leak clue.

bio: Private security contractor who vanishes while tailing a dockside lead linked to Mira's network. Keeps redundant gear caches and minimal trust.
notes: Missing person dossier entry with strong connective tissue to the fraud arc.

bio: Local reporter who follows sealed memos, visitor slips, and copied routing tags long after everyone else stops asking questions.
notes: Her disappearance directly forces the final leak reveal.

bio: Desk sergeant and records coordinator with just enough access to move files, pre-print routing slips, and hide the movement inside normal overnight workflow.
notes: Primary internal leak culprit. Her access explains the restricted-information clues seeded in both department arcs.

bio: Night archive supervisor for North Quay Civil Archive. Habitually obedient to process, until process leaves him alone with sealed transfer tubes and the wrong questions.
notes: Key secondary dossier entry for the C030 interrogation chain.
